title: papaver lapeyrouseanum subsp. endressii
intro: Papaver lapeyrouseanum subsp. endressii, also known as Himalayan poppy, is a rare and exotic species of flowering plant native to the Himalayas, belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The health benefits of Himalayan poppy include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation and alleviate respiratory issues.
